seo.title: present
issues: meta description contains 'AI-powered' which is in the copy forbidden_phrases list — consider rewording to match voice charter

performance.font_notes: next/font/google with Geist + Geist_Mono, latin subset only — zero render-blocking font requests, proper swap via CSS variables
performance.estimated_lcp: good — LCP element is the h1 headline text, no images or heavy assets on either page
cls_risks: Upload page error banner uses animate-[fadeIn] which inserts content below the button — pushes page content down causing minor CLS. Consider reserving space or using an overlay/toast instead.
Chat page EmptyState disappears when first message is sent — the layout shift from empty→messages is unavoidable but the transition is instant, acceptable.
bundle_concerns: @phosphor-icons/react is imported per-icon (UploadSimple, FilePdf, X, etc.) which is correct for tree-shaking — no issue here

Both page.tsx files are 'use client' — entire pages are client components. The upload page could potentially have a server component wrapper with the client drop-zone nested, but for a 2-page app the impact is negligible.
No dynamic imports used, but nothing warrants lazy-loading at this scale

accessibility.semantic_html_notes: Upload page uses <main>, chat page uses <header> and role='log' for message area. Good structure.
accessibility.aria_labels_notes: Drop zone has aria-label, remove button has aria-label, send button has dynamic aria-label, back link has aria-label, message area has role='log' + aria-live='polite', streaming has sr-only text, loading state has sr-only text
issues: Hidden file input has aria-hidden='true' but no associated visible label element — acceptable since the drop zone serves as the label
Chat page textarea has no <label> element — only placeholder text. Add a sr-only <label> for screen readers.
Starter chips in EmptyState have no aria-label explaining they are suggested questions — the text content is self-descriptive so this is minor
Chat page header uses <h2> without a preceding <h1> — heading hierarchy skip

copy_match.notes: All copy in components matches stage-2b-copy.json exactly — headline, subhead, button labels, error messages, aria labels, empty state, and chips all match the copywriter deliverable.

recommendations: Add a sr-only <label> for the chat textarea (e.g., 'Ask a question about your document')
Add an <h1> to the chat page (visually hidden if needed) to fix heading hierarchy — currently jumps from none to <h2>
Rephrase meta description to remove 'AI-powered' per the voice charter forbidden phrases. Suggested: 'Upload a PDF and ask questions about it. Get instant answers from your documents through chat.'
Reserve vertical space for the error banner on the upload page (or use position:absolute) to prevent layout shift when errors appear
Consider adding viewport meta export in layout.tsx for completeness — Next.js handles this automatically but an explicit export documents intent
